2604.10389.title: BLUEmed: Retrieval-Augmented Multi-Agent Debate for Clinical Error Detection
2604.10389.paper_summary: Terminology substitution errors in clinical notes, where one medical term is replaced by a linguistically valid but clinically different term, pose a persistent challenge for automated error detection in healthcare. We introduce BLUEmed, a multi-agent debate framework augmented with hybrid Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) that combines evidence-grounded reasoning with multi-perspective verification for clinical error detection. BLUEmed decomposes each clinical note into focused sub-queries, retrieves source-partitioned evidence through dense, sparse, and online retrieval, and assigns two domain expert agents distinct knowledge bases to produce independent analyses; when the experts disagree, a structured counter-argumentation round and cross-source adjudication resolve the conflict, followed by a cascading safety layer that filters common false-positive patterns. We evaluate BLUEmed on a clinical terminology substitution detection benchmark under both zero-shot and few-shot prompting with multiple backbone models spanning proprietary and open-source families. Experimental results show that BLUEmed achieves the best accuracy (69.13%), ROC-AUC (74.45%), and PR-AUC (72.44%) under few-shot prompting, outperforming both single-agent RAG and debate-only baselines. Further analyses across six backbone models and two prompting strategies confirm that retrieval augmentation and structured debate are complementary, and that the framework benefits most from models with sufficient instruction-following and clinical language understanding.
2604.10389.paper_summary_zh: 臨床筆記中的術語替換錯誤，即一個醫學術語被語言上有效但臨床上不同的術語所替代，對於醫療保健中的自動錯誤檢測構成持續的挑戰。<lb/>我們介紹了BLUEmed，一個多代理辯論框架，增強了混合檢索增強生成（RAG），將基於證據的推理與多角度驗證結合起來以進行臨床錯誤檢測。<lb/>BLUEmed將每個臨床筆記分解為聚焦的子查詢，通過密集、稀疏和在線檢索獲取源分區證據，並為兩個領域專家代理分配不同的知識庫以產生獨立的分析；當專家意見不合時，結構化的反駁回合和跨來源裁決解決衝突，隨後是一個層層安全層，過濾常見的假陽性模式。<lb/>我們在一個臨床術語替換檢測基準上評估BLUEmed，使用多個主幹模型進行零樣本和少樣本提示，這些模型涵蓋專有和開源系列。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，BLUEmed在少樣本提示下達到了最佳準確率（69.13%）、ROC-AUC（74.45%）和PR-AUC（72.44%），超越了單代理RAG和僅辯論的基準。<lb/>對六個主幹模型和兩種提示策略的進一步分析確認了檢索增強和結構化辯論是互補的，並且該框架最受益於具有足夠指令遵循和臨床語言理解的模型。
2604.10389.author: Saukun Thika You et.al.
2604.10389.authors: Saukun Thika You, Nguyen Anh Khoa Tran, Wesley K. Marizane, Hanshu Rao, Qiunan Zhang, Xiaolei Huang
